Instrucciones: Lea atentamente cada pregunta antes de responder. Sea claro y preciso en sus respuestas, utilice lápiz de pasta negro o azul. Tiempo máximo de desarrollo de la prueba 70 minutos.
Criterio de Evaluación de un 60% de exigencia, máximo 30 puntos, para nota 4.0, son 18 puntos.
PART 1 — READING: Signs and Notices (6 points)

Questions 1-6
Which notice (A, B, C, D or E) says this?
For each question, choose the correct letter (A-E).

1. You must be careful with these small parts.

A) CAUTION: Low-temperature soldering required for these components
B) CAUTION: Sensitive electronic components - Handle with care
C) Measure current carefully with the digital meter
D) KEEP DRY - Do not expose to moisture
E) WATCH REPAIR - Bring your watch to reception

2. This device should not get wet.

A) KEEP DRY - Do not expose to moisture
B) OVEN - Do not leave unattended while cooking
C) CAUTION: Sensitive electronic components - Handle with care
D) WATCH REPAIR - Bring your watch to reception
E) FIRST AID KIT - Located behind the door

3. You need to wear special protection for your wrists when working here.

A) Use antistatic protection when handling components
B) E-WASTE COLLECTION POINT - Recycle your old electronics here
C) ESD WRIST STRAP REQUIRED - Static-sensitive area
D) MUSIC ROOM - Instruments available for practice
E) CAUTION: Low-temperature soldering required for these components

4. This course will teach you to build electronic circuits.

A) CAUTION: Sensitive electronic components - Handle with care
B) ELECTRONICS WORKSHOP - Soldering & PCB Assembly Course
C) GIFT SHOP - Electronic accessories on sale
D) KEEP DRY - Do not expose to moisture
E) WATCH REPAIR - Bring your watch to reception

5. These old electronic devices must go in a special container.

A) CAUTION: Sensitive electronic components - Handle with care
B) GIFT SHOP - Electronic accessories on sale
C) E-WASTE COLLECTION POINT - Recycle your old electronics here
D) CAUTION: Low-temperature soldering required for these components
E) Confirm your answer before moving to the next item

6. Do not use a lot of heat when connecting these parts.

A) Use antistatic protection when handling components
B) CAUTION: Low-temperature soldering required for these components
C) Use technical vocabulary from the current unit
D) OVEN - Do not leave unattended while cooking
E) KEEP DRY - Do not expose to moisture
PART 2 — READING: Matching (7 points)

Questions 7-13
Read the text below about three students.
For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B, C, D or E).

Valentina, Diego and Francisca are studying electronics at a technical school.

Valentina is passionate about programming microcontrollers. She has already built several projects using Arduino boards at home. She likes to read documentation online, which is almost always in English. Her latest project is a temperature sensor that sends data to her phone.

Diego is more interested in the hardware side. He loves soldering components onto circuit boards and is very skilled at reading electronic schematics. He wants to work in a factory that manufactures printed circuit boards (PCBs). He practices reading component datasheets in English.

Francisca is interested in telecommunications. She wants to understand how mobile phone networks and internet systems work. She has been learning about radio frequencies and signal processing. She watches English tutorials about networking and communication systems.

7. Who has built projects with Arduino at home?

A) Camila
B) Valentina
C) Diego
D) Matias
E) Fernanda

8. Who is interested in mobile phone networks?

A) Camila
B) Diego
C) Valentina
D) Francisca
E) Matias

9. Who is very good at soldering?

A) Camila
B) Valentina
C) Matias
D) Diego
E) Fernanda

10. Who reads online documentation in English?

A) Camila
B) Diego
C) Matias
D) Valentina
E) Fernanda

11. Who wants to work in PCB manufacturing?

A) Camila
B) Valentina
C) Diego
D) Matias
E) Fernanda

12. Who studies signal processing?

A) Camila
B) Diego
C) Valentina
D) Francisca
E) Matias

13. Who has a project that sends data to a phone?

A) Camila
B) Valentina
C) Diego
D) Matias
E) Fernanda
PART 3 — READING: Comprehension (5 points)

Questions 14-18
Read the text below and choose the correct answer (A, B, C, D or E) for each question.

What is a Printed Circuit Board (PCB)?

A printed circuit board, or PCB, is a flat board made of insulating material (usually fiberglass) with thin copper tracks on its surface. These copper tracks connect electronic components to each other, replacing the need for individual wires.

PCBs are found in almost every electronic device: phones, computers, televisions, medical equipment, and even cars. Without PCBs, modern electronics would not exist.

The process of making a PCB starts with a design. Engineers use special software (like Eagle or KiCad) to draw the circuit layout. Then, the design is transferred to the board using a chemical process called etching, which removes unwanted copper.

After the board is ready, components are placed on it. This can be done by hand (for prototypes) or by machines (for mass production). The components are then soldered to the board to create permanent connections.

Quality control is the final step. Technicians inspect the PCB visually and use testing equipment to make sure all connections work correctly. If there is a defect, the board must be repaired or discarded.

14. What material are most PCBs made of?

A) Designing the circuit layout
B) It removes unwanted copper.
C) Quality control and testing
D) Plastic and aluminium
E) Fiberglass with copper tracks

15. What is the first step in making a PCB?

A) Test the circuit on a breadboard first
B) Soldering the components
C) Designing the circuit layout
D) Testing the connections
E) It paints the board green.

16. What does the etching process do?

A) It removes unwanted copper.
B) Designing the circuit layout
C) It paints the board green.
D) Soldering the components
E) Testing the connections

17. How are components placed on PCBs in mass production?

A) By machines
B) Soldering the components
C) Buying the components
D) Use antistatic protection when handling components
E) By hand

18. What is the last step in PCB production?

A) Designing the circuit layout
B) Designing the circuit
C) Buying the components
D) It paints the board green.
E) Quality control and testing
PART 4 — READING: Vocabulary (6 points)

Questions 19-24
Read the text below and choose the correct word (A, B, C, D or E) for each space.

Electronic components are very small, so you need to (19) _____ carefully when soldering.

A) work     B) solder     C) assemble     D) connect     E) test

A (20) _____ is used to see very small components and connections on a PCB.

A) telescope     B) mirror     C) sing     D) magnifying glass     E) solder

Before touching electronic components, you should discharge any (21) _____ electricity from your body.

A) solder     B) assemble     C) connect     D) test     E) static

The Arduino microcontroller needs to be (22) _____ with the correct code to work.

A) work     B) solder     C) programmed     D) assemble     E) connect

A multimeter can measure voltage, current, and (23) _____.

A) resistance     B) temperature     C) weight     D) solder     E) assemble

Soldering requires a special iron that heats up to a very high (24) _____.

A) speed     B) price     C) sing     D) solder     E) temperature

PART 5 — READING: Gap Fill (6 points)

Questions 25-30
Read the text below and write the missing word for each space.
Use only ONE word for each space.

25. A diode allows electricity to flow in only one _____.

26. You need a soldering _____ to connect components to a PCB.

27. The LED will not work if it is connected the wrong way _____.

28. This sensor _____ designed to measure temperature.

29. The technician tested _____ circuit before connecting it to the power supply.

30. Electronic waste should be recycled _____ reduce pollution.

— Fin de la evaluación — Revisa tus respuestas antes de entregar. —
